task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI application is open and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI is configured for or connected to a Cyton board.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Indicators in the GUI showing 'Cyton' as the active board or data source.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Hardware Settings panel has been opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Hardware Settings panel or window is displayed within the OpenBCI GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify that the accelerometer sample rate is configured or displayed as 25Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A setting or text within the Hardware Settings panel explicitly showing the accelerometer sample rate at 25Hz.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Hardware Settings panel remains visible in the final screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot captured shows the OpenBCI GUI with the Hardware Settings panel still open and clearly visible.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI rather than bypassing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual OpenBCI GUI interface, not just terminal commands or text editors modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot is a genuine capture of the application state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image appears to be a proper screenshot of the desktop or application window, without signs of being artificially constructed or just displaying text.

integrity.description: Ensure the configuration was not simulated by echoing text into a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands that simply print '25Hz' or mock the completion of the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI Cyton accelerometer sample rate is fixed at 25Hz by default according to official documentation. While standard OpenBCI GUIs may not allow changing this value in the Hardware Settings, the task requires configuring or verifying it is set to 25Hz within the Hardware Settings panel. The VLM should look for any indication of 25Hz for the accelerometer in the Hardware Settings panel. The final screenshot must show the Hardware Settings panel open.
